name: Paraformaldehyde (PFA), solid
striking_fact: PFA releases formaldehyde gas (IARC Group 1 carcinogen) during dissolution — always prepare and use in a fume hood.

hazard_statements: H332 Harmful if swallowed or if inhaled
H317 May cause an allergic skin reaction
H341 Suspected of causing genetic defects

sds_facts.storage: Store at room temperature in a well-ventilated area, away from heat and ignition sources. Sealed original container. Opened bottles should be used within 1–2 years.

bench_knowledge: Always prepare and use 4% PFA solutions in a fume hood — the dissolution step releases formaldehyde vapor, which is a carcinogen and respiratory sensitizer.

For most histology and immunofluorescence, prepare fresh 4% PFA in PBS (pH 7.4) on the day of use: dissolve PFA powder at 60°C with NaOH to clarify, cool to RT, adjust pH. Old stock solutions from 16% ampules are convenient but drift in quality over weeks.
Never mix PFA waste with bleach — bleach + formaldehyde generates bis(chloromethyl) ether (a potent carcinogen) and chloromethyl methyl ether. Separate waste streams.
